mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Support for Microsoft Identity platform with Azure provider
mission.description: Support for Microsoft Identity platform with Azure provider. Requested by 38+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
At the moment the Azure provider supports only Azure Active Directory (v1.0) endpoints.
Version v1.0 is deprecated by Microsoft and not fully compliant with the OIDC protocol.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for Microsoft Identity platform with Azure provider
steps.description:
I tried your way to configure
```yaml
AADSTS901002: The 'resource' request parameter is not supported.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for Microsoft Identity platform with Azure provider" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy/issues/1231
codeSnippets: AADSTS901002: The 'resource' request parameter is not supported.

provider: azure
azure-tenant: [REDACTED]
oidc-issuer-url: https://login.microsoftonline.com/[REDACTED]/v2.0
resource: 6dae42f8-4368-4678-94ff-3960e28e3630
set-xauthrequest: "true"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
